name: datefinder
documentation:
# datefinder - extract dates from text

```{image} https://github.com/akoumjian/datefinder/actions/workflows/python-package.yml/badge.svg
:alt: Build Status
:target: https://github.com/akoumjian/datefinder
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/dm/datefinder.svg
:alt: pypi downloads per day
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/datefinder/
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/datefinder.svg
:alt: pypi version
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/datefinder
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/conda/v/conda-forge/datefinder?color=blue&logo=anaconda
:alt: conda version
:target: https://anaconda.org/conda-forge/datefinder
```

A python module for locating dates inside text. Use this package to extract all sorts
of date like strings from a document and turn them into datetime objects.

This module finds the likely datetime strings and then uses
`dateutil` to convert to the datetime object.

## Installation

**With pip**

```sh
pip install datefinder
```

**With conda**

```sh
conda install -c conda-forge datefinder
```

## How to Use

```python
In [1]: string_with_dates = """
   ...: ...
   ...: entries are due by January 4th, 2017 at 8:00pm
   ...: ...
   ...: created 01/15/2005 by ACME Inc. and associates.
   ...: ...
   ...: """

In [2]: import datefinder

In [3]: matches = datefinder.find_dates(string_with_dates)

In [4]: for match in matches:
   ...:     print match
   ...:
2017-01-04 20:00:00
2005-01-15 00:00:00
```

## Demo

- 🎞️ [Video demo] by Calmcode.io. :star:

[video demo]: https://calmcode.io/shorts/datefinder.py.html

modules.name: datefinder.date_fragment
documentation:
This class describes big chunks of text that may contain date strings
Each chunk includes of one of more tokens
Each token is build upon DATE_REGEX matches

functions.name: datefinder.find_dates
documentation:
Extract datetime strings from text

```
param text

A string that contains one or more natural language or literal
datetime strings

type text

str|unicode

param source

Return the original string segment

type source

boolean

param index

Return the indices where the datetime string was located in text

type index

boolean

param strict

Only return datetimes with complete date information. For example:
 of  will not return datetimes.
 will return datetimes.

type strict

boolean

param base_date

Set a default base datetime when parsing incomplete dates

type base_date

datetime

param first

Whether to interpret the the first value in an ambiguous 3-integer date
(01/02/03) as the month, day, or year. Values can be , , .
Default is .

type first

str|unicode

return

Returns a generator that produces  objects,
or a tuple with the source text and index, if requested
```

methods.name: datefinder.DateFinder._find_and_replace
documentation:
---
param date_string: true
return: date_string, tz_string
warning: when multiple tz matches exist the last sorted capture will trump
---

methods.name: datefinder.DateFinder._add_tzinfo
documentation:
take a naive datetime and add dateutil.tz.tzinfo object

```
param datetime_obj

naive datetime object

return

datetime object with tzinfo
```

methods.name: datefinder.DateFinder.extract_date_strings
documentation:
Scans text for possible datetime strings and extracts them
\:param strict: Strict mode will only return dates sourced with day, month, and year

methods.name: datefinder.DateFinder.extract_date_strings_inner
documentation:
Extends extract_date_strings by text_start parameter: used in recursive calls to
store true text coordinates in output

methods.name: datefinder.DateFinder.tokenize_string
documentation:
Get matches from source text. Method merge_tokens will later compose
potential date strings out of these matches.
\:param text: source text like 'the big fight at 2p.m. mountain standard time on ufc.com'
\:return: [(match_text, match_group, {match.capturesdict()}), ...]

methods.name: datefinder.DateFinder.merge_tokens
documentation:
Makes potential date strings out of matches, got from tokenize_string method.
\:param tokens: [(match_text, match_group, {match.capturesdict()}), ...]
\:return: potential date strings
